channel.title: Kasia Zalewska
channel.description: My blog about embedded systems. Personal thoughts, experiments, and notes on RTOS, ARM microcontrollers, and programming

item.title: Blinking an LED on Arduino Uno R4 using direct register access
item.description: Skip Arduino libraries and blink an LED on Arduino Uno R4 using direct registers access. We'll read RA4M1 datasheet and configure a GPIO pin manually.

item.title: Real Embedded Systems: Mastering the ARM Cortex-M4 using Arduino R4
item.description: Master professional embedded systems with the ARM Cortex-M4 (RA4M1). Go beyond the Arduino API: learn registers, RTOS, and bare-metal coding on the Arduino UNO R4.

item.title: Automating the Arduino build process: Arduino CLI and Makefiles
item.description: Moving away from the Arduino IDE to the terminal. A practical guide on using Arduino CLI and Makefiles to handle compilation and build automation for a more efficient embedded workflow.

item.title: Reverse engineering the Arduino build process: from a sketch to a binary
item.description: Let's stop pretending that Arduino sketches are magic written in Arduino language. I'm reverse engineering the Arduino build process, showing the common C/C++ build steps and Arduino specific additions.

item.title: Running Zephyr RTOS on Arduino UNO R4 WiFi
item.description: Turn your ARM-based Arduino board into a high-performance, secure and professional hardware by running Zephyr RTOS on it.

item.title: Resources for learning embedded systems
item.description: Learning resources covering embedded systems, ARM microcontrollers, and C and C++ programming topics.

item.title: cmake-init: the best way to kick off a new C or C++ project
item.description: If you're tired of setting up the same boilerplates for testing, CI/CD, and linting every time you start a new repo, you should probably just use cmake-init tool.

item.title: Adding CI/CD to Arduino projects: Github Actions and Wokwi simulation
item.description: Learn how to automate your Arduino projects using GitHub Actions and Wokwi. This guide shows you how to set up a CI/CD pipeline for automated builds and simulations.
